title: Govt amends norms for exporters availing the interest subvention support

These changes aim to streamline benefits for trade and banks.

title: India-China border trade via Lipulekh Pass to resume after six years
description: Border trade between India and China via Lipulekh Pass in Pithoragarh is set to resume. Preparations are underway for the trade session, typically from June to September. title: Spice exporters brace for tough times as US buyers stay away
description: American buyers have stopped contacting Indian spice exporters. This is due to the Iran war. This trade disruption affects India's valuable farm exports. The US is India's largest spice market. This situation could lead to excess domestic supply and lower prices. Exporters and farmers face increased stress. Trade routes are disrupted, and freight costs are high.
one_line_insight: The disruption in spice exports due to US buyers' withdrawal poses significant risks to Indian exporters and farmers, leading to potential price declines.
signal_reason: The ongoing Iran war has created trade disruptions, affecting the largest market for Indian spices, which could lead to oversupply and lower prices.

title: Anti dumping duty suggested on spandex, raw material for foam imported from China, Vietnam

The proposed duties aim to protect domestic industries from unfair pricing.
